message:
test(indexer/semantic): pin backfill scheduler decision branches with unit tests

Add unit-test coverage for the scheduler-decision function added in
the earlier commit. Each test drives
`semantic_backfill_scheduler_decision_for_capacity` with specific
policy + signals + capacity and asserts the resulting state, reason,
scaled batch size, and back-off hint. This nails every decision
branch so future tweaks to the ordering or the capacity-scaling
formula can't silently drift.

Test scaffolding:
- `default_scheduler_signals()` returns
  `SemanticBackfillSchedulerSignals { foreground_pressure: false,
  lexical_repair_active: false, force: false, operator_disabled:
  false }` — the "clean idle box" baseline used as the starting
  point for each scenario via struct-update syntax.

Scenarios:
- `semantic_backfill_scheduler_runs_and_scales_batch_under_idle_budget`:
  requested=64, capacity=80 → expect Running /
  IdleBudgetAvailable, scheduled_batch_conversations=51 (64*80/100
  clamped to max-1, min-requested). Also asserts
  current_capacity_pct round-trips and next_eligible_after_ms is 0
  for Running.
- `semantic_backfill_scheduler_yields_to_foreground_and_lexical_pressure`:
  Two sub-cases — foreground_pressure=true and
  lexical_repair_active=true — each at capacity=100.
  Both must produce state=Paused with the matching reason
  (ForegroundPressure / LexicalRepairActive), and
  next_eligible_after_ms equal to
  `policy.idle_delay_seconds * 1000` so the consumer knows how long
  to sleep before re-asking.

Together with the types introduced in the same slice, these tests
lock the scheduler semantics before any caller wires it in for
real.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
